label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 13

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won uphill sprint finish after Trek-Segafredo intentionally raised pace to eliminate pure sprinters, following Pascal Ackermann's ultra-long leadout.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo preemptively hardened race mid-stage despite having sprinter, revealing confidence in Pedersen's diesel finisher profile over pure speed.
Headwind under 10km caused extreme compaction and hesitation, making final corner positioning more critical than sustained lead-out trains.
Ackermann's exceptionally long attack hitting climb first created slipstream others exploited, inadvertently serving as leadout for stronger finisher Pedersen.

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Trek's aggressive pre-finish tactics confirm team views him competitive on 7% ramps, not just rolling finishes
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: went first on climb but faded, suggesting tactical misjudgment or insufficient power for 7% gradients at race speed
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: fourth place from Bahrain on punchy uphill finish signals emerging capability beyond pure sprint stages
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: neutral: Bora lead-out invested heavily but Van Poppel finished fourth, question whether resource allocation matched rider's climbing capacity
